channel.title: Andy熱愛的新聞小知識
channel.description: 每日一則知識型話題，用聽的吸收最新資訊。科技趨勢、健康醫學、科普知識，通通在這裡。
channel.itunes:author: Julie Wang

item.title: EP1: 2026美國飲食指南爭議 — 為什麼營養學家說它錯了幾十年？
item.description: 2026年美國飲食指南引發爭議！心臟科醫生指出官方建議存在五大問題。從食物金字塔到最新指南，美國93%成年人代謝不健康，問題可能不在我們吃太多，而是政府告訴我們該吃的東西從根本上就錯了。
item.content:encoded:
2026年美國飲食指南引發爭議！心臟科醫生指出官方建議存在五大問題。從食物金字塔到最新指南，美國93%成年人代謝不健康，問題可能不在我們吃太多，而是政府告訴我們該吃的東西從根本上就錯了。

這集我們聊：美國飲食指南的歷史爭議、三項頂尖臨床試驗（PREDIMED、DIRECT、VIRTA）、2026指南的五個核心問題、以及你該如何避開官方指南的誤區。

item.title: EP2: The 2026 USDA Dietary Guidelines Controversy
item.description: A cardiologist's viral thread reveals why the USDA Dietary Guidelines may have been wrong for decades. 93% of American adults are metabolically unhealthy - maybe the problem isn't what we're eating too much of, but what the government told us to eat.
item.content:encoded:
A cardiologist's viral thread reveals why the USDA Dietary Guidelines may have been wrong for decades. 93% of American adults are metabolically unhealthy - maybe the problem isn't what we're eating too much of, but what the government told us to eat.

In this episode: the history of USDA guidelines, the PREDIMED/DIRECT/VIRTA trials, five core problems with the 2026 guidelines, and practical advice for healthier eating.

item.title: EP3: GLP-1 減肥藥的隱藏成本 — 你瘦了，但骨頭也瘦了嗎？
item.description: GLP-1 減肥藥（Ozempic、Wegovy）讓數百萬人成功瘦身，但 Wake Forest 大學最新研究警告——快速減重可能伴隨骨質流失。年長使用者、停經後女性風險更高。
item.content:encoded:
GLP-1 減肥藥（Ozempic、Wegovy）讓數百萬人成功瘦身，但 Wake Forest 大學最新研究警告——快速減重可能伴隨骨質流失。年長使用者、停經後女性風險更高。

這集我們聊：為什麼快速減重會傷害骨頭、Wake Forest 研究核心發現、高風險族群、以及如何在減重同時保護骨骼健康。

item.title: EP3 (English): The Hidden Cost of GLP-1 Drugs — Losing Weight, Losing Bone?
item.description: GLP-1 drugs (Ozempic, Wegovy) helped millions slim down, but Wake Forest research warns rapid weight loss may come with bone loss. Older users and post-menopausal women are at higher risk.
item.content:encoded: GLP-1 drugs (Ozempic, Wegovy) helped millions slim down, but Wake Forest research warns rapid weight loss may come with bone loss. Older users and post-menopausal women are at higher risk.

item.title: EP4: GPT-5.6 來了！OpenAI 最新模型如何用健康智能改變你的生活？
item.description: OpenAI 推出 GPT-5.6 三款模型（Sol/Terra/Luna），分層定價、健康智能、Sol Ultra 破解 50 年數學難題。AI 從奢侈品變日用品。
item.content:encoded: OpenAI 推出 GPT-5.6 三款模型（Sol/Terra/Luna），分層定價、健康智能、Sol Ultra 破解 50 年數學難題。AI 從奢侈品變日用品。

item.title: EP4 (English): GPT-5.6 Is Here! How OpenAI's New Models Reshape Your Life with Health Intelligence
item.description: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 with three models (Sol/Terra/Luna), tiered pricing, Health Intelligence, and Sol Ultra cracking a 50-year math problem. AI goes from luxury to everyday tool.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 with three models (Sol/Terra/Luna), tiered pricing, Health Intelligence, and Sol Ultra cracking a 50-year math problem. AI goes from luxury to everyday tool.

item.title: EP5: Apple 告 OpenAI 內幕有多勁爆？400名前員工、偷零件面試、後門入侵伺服器
item.description: Apple 正式起訴 OpenAI，指控系統性竊取硬體商業機密。超過 400 名前員工已在 OpenAI 工作，訴訟揭露令人難以置信的內幕。
item.content:encoded: Apple 正式起訴 OpenAI，指控系統性竊取硬體商業機密。超過 400 名前員工已在 OpenAI 工作，訴訟揭露令人難以置信的內幕。

item.title: EP5 (English): Inside Apple's Lawsuit Against OpenAI — 400 Ex-Employees, Stolen Parts, Backdoor Server Access
item.description: Apple sued OpenAI, accusing systematic theft of hardware trade secrets. Over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI; the suit reveals jaw-dropping details.
item.content:encoded: Apple sued OpenAI, accusing systematic theft of hardware trade secrets. Over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI; the suit reveals jaw-dropping details.

item.title: EP6: 骨頭也有荷爾蒙？科學家發現骨骼—血糖全新調控機制
item.description: 你的骨頭不只是支架——最新科學發現骨骼分泌骨鈣素，直接調控胰島素分泌和血糖代謝。骨質疏鬆和糖尿病的關聯比你想像的更近。
item.content:encoded: 你的骨頭不只是支架——最新科學發現骨骼分泌骨鈣素，直接調控胰島素分泌和血糖代謝。骨質疏鬆和糖尿病的關聯比你想像的更近。

item.title: EP6 (English): Your Bones Make Hormones? The Bone–Blood Sugar Control Link
item.description: New science shows your skeleton secretes osteocalcin, a hormone that directly regulates insulin secretion and blood-sugar metabolism. The link between osteoporosis and diabetes is closer than you think.
item.content:encoded: New science shows your skeleton secretes osteocalcin, a hormone that directly regulates insulin secretion and blood-sugar metabolism. The link between osteoporosis and diabetes is closer than you think.

item.title: EP7: 一個蛀牙如何影響你的心臟？根管治療能降低心臟病風險
item.description: King's College London 研究發現成功的根管治療能顯著改善血糖和膽固醇，降低心臟病風險。口腔健康比你想像的更影響全身。
item.content:encoded: King's College London 研究發現成功的根管治療能顯著改善血糖和膽固醇，降低心臟病風險。口腔健康比你想像的更影響全身。

item.title: EP7 (English): How a Cavity Affects Your Heart — Root Canal Lowers Heart Risk
item.description: King's College London found successful root canal treatment significantly improves blood sugar and cholesterol, lowering heart-disease risk. Oral health affects your whole body more than you think.
item.content:encoded: King's College London found successful root canal treatment significantly improves blood sugar and cholesterol, lowering heart-disease risk. Oral health affects your whole body more than you think.

item.title: EP8: 不再追求更大模型？2026年AI產業三大巨變，你跟上沒？
item.description: 2026年AI產業從追求最大模型，轉向最便宜、最適任務、最可控的系統。Perplexity執行長稱之為編排系統，中國模型用量暴增。
item.content:encoded: 2026年AI產業從追求最大模型，轉向最便宜、最適任務、最可控的系統。Perplexity執行長稱之為編排系統，中國模型用量暴增。

item.title: EP8 (English): Is the AI Race No Longer About Bigger Models?
item.description: In 2026 the AI race shifts from bigger models to cheaper, smarter systems. Perplexity's CEO calls it the orchestration system; China's token usage surges.
item.content:encoded: In 2026 the AI race shifts from bigger models to cheaper, smarter systems. Perplexity's CEO calls it the orchestration system; China's token usage surges.

item.title: EP9: 愛滋病可能被根治？CRISPR 基因編輯首次成功從細胞中剪出 HIV
item.description: 科學家首次用 CRISPR 從人類免疫細胞剪除 HIV 基因。EBT-101 在動物實驗病毒未復發，距離根治邁進一步。
item.content:encoded: 科學家首次用 CRISPR 從人類免疫細胞剪除 HIV 基因。EBT-101 在動物實驗病毒未復發，距離根治邁進一步。

item.title: EP9 (English): Could AIDS Finally Be Cured? CRISPR Cuts HIV Out of Cells
item.description: Scientists used CRISPR to cut HIV genes out of human immune cells for the first time. EBT-101 showed no viral rebound in animal studies.
item.content:encoded: Scientists used CRISPR to cut HIV genes out of human immune cells for the first time. EBT-101 showed no viral rebound in animal studies.

item.title: EP10: AI 科學家來了？《Science》發表通用型生物醫學 AI 代理，自動化研究流程
item.description: 《Science》發表能自主進行生物醫學研究的 AI 代理，從文獻調閱、提出假說到執行實驗貫穿整條流程。
item.content:encoded: 《Science》發表能自主進行生物醫學研究的 AI 代理，從文獻調閱、提出假說到執行實驗貫穿整條流程。

item.title: EP10 (English): Is the AI Scientist Here? Science Publishes a Generalist Biomedical Agent
item.description: Science published a peer-reviewed AI agent that autonomously runs the full biomedical research pipeline, from hypothesis to experiment.
item.content:encoded: Science published a peer-reviewed AI agent that autonomously runs the full biomedical research pipeline, from hypothesis to experiment.

item.title: EP11: 牛津用 AI 設計「萬能疫苗」進入人體試驗：我們離一針打遍所有病毒還有多遠？
item.description: 牛津大學用 AI 設計的萬能疫苗進入人體試驗，瞄準病毒幹部保守區，有望對付一大類病毒。
item.content:encoded: 牛津大學用 AI 設計的萬能疫苗進入人體試驗，瞄準病毒幹部保守區，有望對付一大類病毒。

item.title: EP11 (English): Oxford's AI-Designed "Universal Vaccine" Enters Human Trials
item.description: Oxford's AI-designed universal vaccine entered human trials, targeting the conserved stem region to fight a whole class of viruses.
item.content:encoded: Oxford's AI-designed universal vaccine entered human trials, targeting the conserved stem region to fight a whole class of viruses.

item.title: EP12: 健身房器材背後的硬科技 — 為什麼一台智能器械能取代一整間健身房？
item.description: 現代健身器材的硬科技不在鐵塊本身，而在於用電磁阻力、感測器與演算法，把一整面配重片牆的訓練壓進一面掛在牆上的螢幕。這集帶你看懂配重片、漸進超負荷、渦電流、電刺激與連網化的原理。
item.content:encoded:
現代健身器材的硬科技不在鐵塊本身，而在於用電磁阻力、感測器與演算法，把一整面配重片牆的訓練壓進一面掛在牆上的螢幕。

這集聊：配重片堆的機械工程、漸進式超負載原則、電磁阻力（渦電流）如何取代鐵塊、電刺激肌肉訓練的真相，以及連網化如何讓器材變成數據終端。

item.title: EP12 (English): The Hard Tech Behind Gym Equipment — Why One Smart Machine Can Replace a Whole Gym
item.description: The real hard tech in modern fitness gear isn't the steel plates — it's electromagnetic resistance, sensors, and algorithms packing a whole wall of weight stacks into a wall-mounted screen. This episode covers weight stacks, progressive overload, eddy-current resistance, EMS, and connectivity.
item.content:encoded:
The real hard tech in modern fitness gear isn't the steel plates — it's electromagnetic resistance, sensors, and algorithms packing a whole wall of weight stacks into a wall-mounted screen.

This episode covers the mechanical engineering of weight stacks, the progressive overload principle, how eddy-current resistance replaces iron, the truth about EMS training, and how connectivity turns gear into a data terminal.

item.title: EP13: 香港房租真的漲到歷史新高了嗎？私樓租金五連破頂、公屋輪候卻創新低
item.description: 香港私樓租金在 2026 年屢創新高，但公屋輪候時間卻降到近年新低。這兩件事同時發生，揭露了住房市場最根本的矛盾：供應增加了，卻沒流到最需要的人手上。本集拆解私樓租金、公屋輪候、夾心層與細分單位、土地結構瓶頸，以及最新《基本法住房單位條例》。
item.content:encoded:
香港私樓租金在 2026 年屢創新高，但公屋輪候時間卻降到近年新低。

本集拆解：私樓租金為什麼漲到史上最高、公屋輪候為什麼反而變短、夾心層與細分單位的困境、土地分配的根本瓶頸，以及 2026 年生效的《基本法住房單位條例》。

item.title: EP13 (English): Is Hong Kong Rent Really at an All-Time High? Private Rents Keep Breaking Records While Public Housing Wait Times Hit a New Low
item.description: Hong Kong's private rents posted repeated record highs in 2026, yet public housing wait times dropped to a recent low. Both happening together exposes the core contradiction: supply is rising, but it isn't reaching the people who need it most. This episode breaks down private rents, public housing queues, the sandwich class and subdivided flats, the land bottleneck, and the new Basic Housing Units Ordinance.
item.content:encoded:
Hong Kong's private rents posted repeated record highs in 2026, yet public housing wait times dropped to a recent low.

This episode breaks down: why private rents hit all-time highs, why public waits got shorter, the sandwich class and subdivided flats, the land-distribution bottleneck, and the Basic Housing Units Ordinance effective in 2026.

item.title: EP14: 香港填海真的越填越快嗎？單一工程變大、總量反而變慢，關鍵在這三項科技
item.description: 你感覺香港填海越填越快，一半真一半錯覺。現代填海科技（深層水泥攪拌 DCM、動態定位挖泥船）讓單一工程更快更大，但香港累計填海總量在九〇年代新市鎮高峰後已放緩，維港內幾乎停填，明日大嶼也擱置。本集拆解百年填海史與關鍵科技。
item.content:encoded:
你感覺香港填海越填越快，一半真一半錯覺。

本集拆解：百年填海史（從西漢鹽田到赤鱲角機場）、為什麼感覺變快（工程規模變大 + DCM/動態定位船等科技）、三道煞車（維港條例、明日大嶼擱置、環保監督），以及「單一工程快 ≠ 總量多」的核心區分。

item.title: EP14 (English): Is Hong Kong Really Reclaiming Land Faster Than Ever? Bigger Single Projects, Slower Total — and Three Key Technologies
item.description: Your feeling that Hong Kong is reclaiming land faster is half true, half illusion. Modern tech (deep cement mixing, dynamically positioned dredgers) makes single projects faster and bigger, but cumulative reclamation has slowed since the 1990s peak, harbour reclamation nearly stopped, and Lantau Tomorrow is shelved. This episode breaks down a century of reclamation and the key tech.
item.content:encoded:
Your feeling that Hong Kong is reclaiming land faster is half true, half illusion.

This episode breaks down: a century of reclamation (from Western Han salt pans to Chek Lap Kok airport), why it feels faster (bigger projects + DCM / DP vessels), three brakes (Harbour Ordinance, Lantau Tomorrow shelved, environmental oversight), and the core distinction that "faster single project ≠ more total land."

item.title: EP15: AI 錢到底去哪了？晶片廠狂賺 4300 億，科技巨頭卻燒掉 1.8 兆（雙人主持 Andy+Julie）
item.description: AI 熱潮裡，晶片製造商已經變成現金製造機——未來 12 個月自由現金流預估高達 4300 億美元，是兩年前的三倍；同一時間，科技巨頭的自由現金流卻要首度轉負，因為他們在 2026–2027 年合計要燒掉約 1.8 兆美元的 AI 資本支出。（本集為 Andy 與 Julie 雙人對話版。）
item.content:encoded:
AI 熱潮裡，晶片製造商已經變成「現金製造機」——未來 12 個月自由現金流預估高達 4300 億美元，是兩年前的三倍；同一時間，科技巨頭的自由現金流卻要首度轉負，因為他們在 2026–2027 年合計要燒掉約 1.8 兆美元的 AI 資本支出。

這集用「淘金熱賣鏟子」的比喻，拆解半導體供應鏈如何成為 AI 時代最大贏家，以及為什麼科技巨頭願意虧本也要投入 AI。

item.title: EP15 (English): Where Is the AI Money Going? Chipmakers Are Minting $430 Billion While Tech Giants Burn $1.8 Trillion（雙人主持 Andy+Julie）
item.description: In the AI boom, chipmakers have become cash machines — projected to generate a record $430 billion in free cash flow over the next 12 months, triple what they earned two years ago — while the hyperscalers buying their chips are set to post negative free cash flow for the first time ever.（本集為 Andy 與 Julie 雙人對話版。）
item.content:encoded:
In the AI boom, chipmakers have become cash machines — projected to generate a record $430 billion in free cash flow over the next 12 months, triple what they earned two years ago.

Using the "Gold Rush shovel sellers" analogy, this episode breaks down why the semiconductor supply chain is winning first, and why tech giants are willing to burn cash on AI.

item.title: EP16: 美銀為什麼把 Coinbase 目標價砍到 203 美元？加密貨幣的華爾街之路為何跌跌撞撞（雙人主持 Andy+Julie）
item.description: 美銀把 Coinbase 目標價下調至 203 美元，表面是對單一公司的獲利擔憂，背後反映的是整個加密貨幣走向華爾街規範化過程中的結構性陣痛——監管不確定、手續費收入承壓、以及傳統金融與幣圈文化難以真正融合。（本集為 Andy 與 Julie 雙人對話版。）
item.content:encoded:
美銀（Bank of America）將 Coinbase 目標價下調至 203 美元，表面是對單一公司的獲利擔憂，背後反映的其實是整個「加密貨幣走向華爾街規範化」過程中的結構性陣痛。

這集拆解 Coinbase 怎麼賺錢、為什麼華爾街對加密貨幣態度搖擺，以及目標價下調真正代表什麼。

item.title: EP16 (English): Why Did Bank of America Cut Coinbase's Target Price to $203? Crypto's Bumpy Road to Wall Street（雙人主持 Andy+Julie）
item.description: Bank of America cutting Coinbase's target price to $203 looks like a single-stock call, but it really reflects the structural growing pains of crypto's march toward Wall Street normalization — regulatory uncertainty, compressed fee revenue, and the gap between traditional finance and crypto culture.（本集為 Andy 與 Julie 雙人對話版。）
item.content:encoded:
Bank of America cutting Coinbase's target price to $203 looks like a single-stock call, but it really reflects the structural growing pains of crypto's march toward Wall Street normalization.

This episode breaks down how Coinbase earns, why Wall Street keeps waffling on crypto, and what a target-price cut actually means.

item.title: EP17: 韓國股市為何一天暴跌 8%？槓桿泡沫破裂全解析
item.description: KOSPI 單日暴跌 8.18%，三星電子跌 9.3%、SK 海力士跌 9.5%。表面上是 AI 股獲利了結，但深層原因更驚人——韓國散戶的融資槓桿已經到了荒謬的程度，一家半導體 ETF 高達 70% 的資金是借來的。本集拆解韓國股市暴跌的結構性原因、槓桿泡沫如何破裂，以及這場危機對所有投資人的啟示。（本集為 Andy 與 Julie 雙人對話版。）
item.content:encoded:
KOSPI 單日暴跌 8.18%，三星電子和 SK 海力士同步重挫。表面上是 AI 股估值過高引發獲利了結，但韓國背後還有更瘋狂的故事——散戶瘋狂借錢買股票的「債務投資」文化。

本集拆解：韓國如何從全球最熱的 AI 晶片市場，變成一天觸發熔斷的崩盤現場？融資斷頭的連鎖反應如何運作？三倍槓桿 ETF 在暴跌時會有多慘？以及對一般投資人的四個重要啟示。

item.title: EP17 (English): Why Did South Korea's Stock Market Crash 8%?
item.description: KOSPI crashed over 8% in a single day. Samsung -9.3%, SK Hynix -9.5%. While AI stock valuations triggered the selloff, Korea's unique "debt investing" culture turned it into a full-blown crisis — with some semiconductor ETFs having 70% of their funds borrowed on margin. This duo episode breaks down the crash, the leverage bubble, and 4 key lessons for every investor.
item.content:encoded:
South Korea's KOSPI index plunged 8.18%. Samsung Electronics fell 9.3%, SK Hynix fell 9.5%. On the surface it looks like an AI stock correction, but Korea's structural problems run much deeper.

Hosts Andy and Julie break down Korea's "debt investing" frenzy, how margin calls create cascading liquidations, why 3X leveraged ETFs amplify crashes, and the four most important lessons for investors everywhere.

item.title: EP18: 台積電營收創歷史新高，但 AI 巨頭為何反而現金流轉負？
item.description: AI 基礎建設狂潮下，台積電 Q2 營收創 1.27 兆台幣新高，但五大科技巨頭的自由現金流卻首度轉負——誰在賺錢誰在燒錢？
item.content:encoded: AI 基礎建設狂潮下，台積電 Q2 營收創 1.27 兆台幣新高，但五大科技巨頭的自由現金流卻首度轉負——誰在賺錢誰在燒錢？

item.title: EP18 (English): TSMC Revenue Hits Record High, But Why Are AI Giants' Cash Flows Turning Negative?
item.description: Amid the AI infrastructure boom, TSMC's Q2 revenue hit a record NT$1.27 trillion, while tech giants' free cash flow turns negative for the first time.
item.content:encoded: Amid the AI infrastructure boom, TSMC's Q2 revenue hit a record NT$1.27 trillion, while tech giants' free cash flow turns negative for the first time.

item.title: EP44: 今天要聊聊 SpaceX 星艦二度試射中止、市值遭 Meta 超車，這到底是不是太空帝國的危險號角？
item.description: 其實，這次失敗並不是末日。星艦延期只是火箭研發的常態——記得 2015 年獵鷹 9 號陸地回收屢次次失敗，市場也曾唱衰，但回收成功後發射成本驟降，奠定今日壟斷。 真正關鍵的是「軌道資料中心」的 AI 敘事能否如期兌現。那是支撐 SpaceX 兆級估值的底層邏輯，而非單枚火箭的成敗。 市場擔心的從來不是一次試射失敗，而是三件事疊加：IPO 後財報透明化揭露「軌道資料中心」仍停留在 PPT；宏觀利率壓力讓高估值成長股首當其衝；競爭敘事轉向，Meta 在 AI 基礎建設的實質營收落地，讓市場重新評估誰才是 AI 軍備競賽的真正贏家。 火箭研發的本質就是「用失敗換數據」。4 具引擎未點火這種問題，屬於可定位、可修復的範疇，而非根本性設計缺陷。 真正的變數在於：當利率維持高位、資金成本變貴，SpaceX 還能燒多久的錢去跑這套「快速試錯」？這才是市值能否回升的底層約束。 對一般人來說，這件事有三個值得關注的信號：AI 基建敘事進入驗證期、太空經濟不再免死，以及分散風險的重要性。 別忘了，星艦決定 SpaceX 能否把發射成本降到極低，進而影響 Starlink 網路擴張速度、太空旅遊價格，以及 NASA 登月時程——間接影響全球通訊與航天產業的競爭格局。 我是 Andy，感謝收看。

item.title: EP44: This week, SpaceX's Starship V3 test aborted at launch—four engines failed to ignite—and the stock has been sliding for seven straight sessions, letting Meta overtake its market cap. Is this the beginning of the end for Musk's space empire?
item.description: Actually, no. This kind of hiccup is par for the course in rocket development. Remember 2015? Falcon 9's landing attempts blew up repeatedly, the market panicked, but once landing worked, launch costs plummeted and the monopoly set in. What really matters isn't a single test—it's whether the "orbital data center" AI narrative can actually materialize. That's what underpins SpaceX's trillion‑dollar valuation, not whether one rocket flies. The market's worry is threefold: post-IPO transparency showed the data‑center vision is still on PowerPoint, high rates are punishing growth stocks, and Meta's real AI‑infrastructure revenue is shifting perceptions of who's winning the AI arms race. Rocket development is about turning failure into data. Four engines not lighting is a locatable, fixable engineering issue—not a design flaw. The real variable? With rates high and money expensive, how long can SpaceX keep burning cash to run this "fail fast" loop? That's the constraint on any valuation rebound. For the rest of us, this story flashes three signals: the AI‑infra narrative is entering verification, space economics loses its immunity, and diversification matters. Remember, Starship decides how low SpaceX can push launch costs—which then affects Starlink rollout speed, space‑tourism pricing, and NASA's lunar timeline—rippling through global comms and aerospace competitiveness. I'm Andy, thanks for listening.

Falcon 9's landing attempts blew up repeatedly, the market panicked, but once landing worked, launch costs plummeted and the monopoly set in. That's what underpins SpaceX's trillion‑dollar valuation, not whether one rocket flies. The market's worry is threefold: post-IPO transparency showed the data‑center vision is still on PowerPoint, high rates are punishing growth stocks, and Meta's real AI‑infrastructure revenue is shifting perceptions of who's winning the AI arms race. Rocket development is about turning failure into data. Four engines not lighting is a locatable, fixable engineering issue—not a design flaw. The real variable? With rates high and money expensive, how long can SpaceX keep burning cash to run this "fail fast" loop? That's the constraint on any valuation rebound. Remember, Starship decides how low SpaceX can push launch costs—which then affects Starlink rollout speed, space‑tourism pricing, and NASA's lunar timeline—rippling through global comms and aerospace competitiveness. I'm Andy, thanks for listening.

item.title: EP45: 今天要聊聊美國擬禁中國開源 AI 模型，以及剛剛登頂的 Kimi K3 是否會成為中美 AI 脫鈎的導火線。
item.description: 最近，美國政府正在研究以行政命令限制中國開源 AI 模型在境內的使用，同時考慮將中國的 AI 實驗室列入實體清單。這背後的擔心是，開源模式可能被不友善的勢力濫用，同時也威脅到美國本土 AI 產業的領先地位。 就在這個敏感時刻，中國 Moonshot AI 研發的 Kimi K3 在 Frontend Code Arena 上以 76% 的勝率奪得第一名，超越了 Claude Fable 5 與 GPT-5.6，而且預計將於 7 月 27 日開源權重。這意味著開發者將能夠免費取得一個性能接近頂尖閉源模型、但成本更低的工具。 如果禁令真的落地，可能會加速全球 AI 生態系統的分裂：美國陣營傾向閉源、合規優先；中國陣營則可能推動開源擴散、自主堆疊。對開發者來說，這不只是理論上的擔憂——未來可能需要維護兩套模型適配層，跨陣營的模型權重難以互通，甚至研究工具鏈也可能出現「語言隔閡」。 當然，純粹的技術封鎖很難徹底。開源權重一旦發布，就難以被完全阻擋。但無論如何，這場爭論提醒我們：AI 的未來不只取決於誰的模型更強，也取決於我們願意多開放還是多保守。 我是 Julie，謝謝收聽。
item.content:encoded: 最近，美國政府正在研究以行政命令限制中國開源 AI 模型在境內的使用，同時考慮將中國的 AI 實驗室列入實體清單。這背後的擔心是，開源模式可能被不友善的勢力濫用，同時也威脅到美國本土 AI 產業的領先地位。 就在這個敏感時刻，中國 Moonshot AI 研發的 Kimi K3 在 Frontend Code Arena 上以 76% 的勝率奪得第一名，超越了 Claude Fable 5 與 GPT-5.6，而且預計將於 7 月 27 日開源權重。這意味著開發者將能夠免費取得一個性能接近頂尖閉源模型、但成本更低的工具。 如果禁令真的落地，可能會加速全球 AI 生態系統的分裂：美國陣營傾向閉源、合規優先；中國陣營則可能推動開源擴散、自主堆疊。對開發者來說，這不只是理論上的擔憂——未來可能需要維護兩套模型適配層，跨陣營的模型權重難以互通，甚至研究工具鏈也可能出現「語言隔閡」。 當然，純粹的技術封鎖很難徹底。開源權重一旦發布，就難以被完全阻擋。但無論如何，這場爭論提醒我們：AI 的未來不只取決於誰的模型更強，也取決於我們願意多開放還是多保守。 我是 Julie，謝謝收聽。

item.title: EP45: Today we're talking about the U.S. considering a ban on Chinese open-source AI models, and how China's just-released Kimi K3 might become the flashpoint in a growing tech divide.
item.description: The Biden administration is looking at an executive order that would block Chinese open-source AI models from running inside the United States, and may even add Chinese AI labs to the Entity List. The concern is two‑fold: open weights could be misused by bad actors, and they threaten the dominance of American AI companies. Just as that debate heats up, Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 has topped the Frontend Code Arena with a 76% win rate—beating Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6—and is set to go open-source on July 27. That means developers worldwide could soon access a high‑performing model at a fraction of the cost of the leading closed‑source alternatives. If the ban moves forward, we could see the global AI ecosystem split into two camps: a U.S.-leaning side that favors closed, compliance‑first models, and a China‑leaning side that pushes open diffusion and self‑stacked solutions. For developers, that might mean maintaining two separate model adapters, dealing with weight incompatibility, and even facing a “language barrier” in research toolchains. Of course, a pure technical blockade is hard to enforce—once the weights are out, they can be mirrored or shared via P2P. But the debate highlights a bigger question: will AI’s future be shaped more by openness or by control? I'm Julie, thanks for listening.
item.content:encoded: The Biden administration is looking at an executive order that would block Chinese open-source AI models from running inside the United States, and may even add Chinese AI labs to the Entity List. The concern is two‑fold: open weights could be misused by bad actors, and they threaten the dominance of American AI companies. Just as that debate heats up, Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 has topped the Frontend Code Arena with a 76% win rate—beating Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6—and is set to go open-source on July 27. That means developers worldwide could soon access a high‑performing model at a fraction of the cost of the leading closed‑source alternatives. If the ban moves forward, we could see the global AI ecosystem split into two camps: a U.S.-leaning side that favors closed, compliance‑first models, and a China‑leaning side that pushes open diffusion and self‑stacked solutions. For developers, that might mean maintaining two separate model adapters, dealing with weight incompatibility, and even facing a “language barrier” in research toolchains. Of course, a pure technical blockade is hard to enforce—once the weights are out, they can be mirrored or shared via P2P. But the debate highlights a bigger question: will AI’s future be shaped more by openness or by control? I'm Julie, thanks for listening.

item.title: EP46: 今天要跟大家分享一則超級酷的太空發現：科學家在銀河系中心的巨大分子雲——人馬座 B2 裡，偵測到了第一個已知的星際糖分子，叫做「赤藓酮糖」。
item.description: 這不只是一種普通的糖，它是生命體合成核糖（RNA 的骨架）的前體之一。也就是說，構成生命的基本有機分子，其實早在太陽系誕生之前，就已經在星際雲中製造出來了。 究竟是怎麼發生的？在接近絕對零度的環境裡，塵埃顆粒的表面成為微型反應爐，宇宙射線和紫外線打斷簡單分子的鍵，讓它們重新組合成更複雜的糖。當這些雲氣隨後塌縮形成恆星和行星時，這些糖就被帶進了新生的天體，也許有一天會落到類似地球的行星表面。 這意味著，生命的前驅物質不是地球獨有的偶然產物，而是宇宙化學的一部分。如果這樣的糖在星際空間很常見，那麼生命在其他地方出現的機會就會大幅提升。 不過，這還不等於外星人真的存在——我們仍然需要更多證據來確認生命是否真的能在那樣的環境裡誕生。但至少現在我们知道，構成生命的基本零件，早在星星點亮之前，就已經在星雲裡「備好了」。 我是 Julie，感謝收聽。
item.content:encoded: 這不只是一種普通的糖，它是生命體合成核糖（RNA 的骨架）的前體之一。也就是說，構成生命的基本有機分子，其實早在太陽系誕生之前，就已經在星際雲中製造出來了。 究竟是怎麼發生的？在接近絕對零度的環境裡，塵埃顆粒的表面成為微型反應爐，宇宙射線和紫外線打斷簡單分子的鍵，讓它們重新組合成更複雜的糖。當這些雲氣隨後塌縮形成恆星和行星時，這些糖就被帶進了新生的天體，也許有一天會落到類似地球的行星表面。 這意味著，生命的前驅物質不是地球獨有的偶然產物，而是宇宙化學的一部分。如果這樣的糖在星際空間很常見，那麼生命在其他地方出現的機會就會大幅提升。 不過，這還不等於外星人真的存在——我們仍然需要更多證據來確認生命是否真的能在那樣的環境裡誕生。但至少現在我们知道，構成生命的基本零件，早在星星點亮之前，就已經在星雲裡「備好了」。 我是 Julie，感謝收聽。

item.title: EP46: Today we’re talking about a sweet discovery from deep space: scientists have found the first known sugar molecule in interstellar space, inside the massive molecular cloud Sagittarius B2 near the center of our galaxy.
item.description: The molecule is called erythrulose—a four‑carbon sugar you can also find in raspberries on Earth. What’s exciting is that it’s a prebiotic building block, meaning it’s part of the chemistry that can lead to life. How does sugar form in something as cold as a nebula? Dust grains act as tiny reaction chambers; ultraviolet light and cosmic rays break simple molecules like water and methanol apart, and then they recombine into more complex organics—including sugars. When the cloud later collapses to form stars and planets, those sugars get trapped in comets and asteroids and can be delivered to newborn worlds. In other words, the raw ingredients for life aren’t unique to Earth—they’re being cooked up in space, long before planets like ours even existed. So the next time you look up at the night sky, remember: between those stars, vast clouds might be quietly brewing the very molecules that make life possible. I’m Julie, thanks for listening.
item.content:encoded: The molecule is called erythrulose—a four‑carbon sugar you can also find in raspberries on Earth. What’s exciting is that it’s a prebiotic building block, meaning it’s part of the chemistry that can lead to life. How does sugar form in something as cold as a nebula? Dust grains act as tiny reaction chambers; ultraviolet light and cosmic rays break simple molecules like water and methanol apart, and then they recombine into more complex organics—including sugars. When the cloud later collapses to form stars and planets, those sugars get trapped in comets and asteroids and can be delivered to newborn worlds. In other words, the raw ingredients for life aren’t unique to Earth—they’re being cooked up in space, long before planets like ours even existed. So the next time you look up at the night sky, remember: between those stars, vast clouds might be quietly brewing the very molecules that make life possible. I’m Julie, thanks for listening.

item.title: EP47: iPhone 18 Face ID 變小 35% 的真正意義是什麼？技術躍進背後的漲價訊號
item.description: Andy+Julie 雙人主持：iPhone 18 的 Face ID 模組將縮小 35%，靠的是 TSMC 全新 2nm 封裝技術與微型化光學感測器，但這項硬體升級也推升了整機成本。雙人聊天式解析這背後的技術與價格邏輯。

item.title: EP48: Can Tesla's EV Business Keep Funding Optimus and Robotaxi Before the Money Runs Out?
item.description: Julie breaks down Tesla's Q2 2026 earnings preview: record 480K deliveries, ballooning Optimus R&D spend, and Robotaxi's regulatory progress vs capital burn. Can the EV business keep both AI dreams alive?
item.content:encoded: Julie breaks down Tesla's Q2 2026 earnings preview: record 480K deliveries, ballooning Optimus R&D spend, and Robotaxi's regulatory progress vs capital burn. Can the EV business keep both AI dreams alive?
